leadText: Togedemaru (Japanese: トゲデマル Togedemaru) is a dual-type Electric/Steel Pokémon introduced in Generation VII. It is not known to evolve into or from any other Pokémon.

sections.title: Biology
sections.text: Biology [ edit | edit source ] Concept art of Togedemaru for Pokémon Sun and Moon Togedemaru is a spherical, rodent-like Pokémon . Most of its front is white, while its back is primarily gray. It has black eyes, a short pointed snout with a black nose, rounded ears, and a circular, yellow marking on each cheek. Scattered across its back are 14 fur spikes that appear as yellow and brown triangular markings. When attacking, surprised, or agitated, these spikes will stand straight up. There is a tail-like needle on the back of its head. The needle is gray with a yellow tip and is shaped like a lightning bolt. Additionally, it has stubby arms and legs. When Togedemaru's spines are out, its cheek marks sometimes look like electrical outlets. Togedemaru uses the needle on top of its head to attract and gather electricity. Groups can be seen gathered with their needles standing upright, waiting to be struck by lightning on stormy days to make up for its somewhat limited electric capacity. Some Trainers use its ability to absorb electricity as a strategy in battle. The opponent is coaxed into unleashing an Electric-type attack, and then has that power turned against it. [ 1 ] When it is pleased, this Pokémon will curl into a ball and roll around uncontrollably. Togedemaru clashes with Elekid whenever it tries to steal the latter's electricity, usually ending with even matches. As mentioned in Sleep Style Dex , Togedemaru usually sleeps while curled up with its spiny fur on its back at rest. Despite being asleep, one can see its fur stand on end for protection if the Pokémon should become agitated. A special Togedemaru in Alola acts as the Totem Pokémon for Sophocles 's trial in Hokulani Observatory . This Togedemaru possesses Z-Power , an energy that originates from Ultra Wormholes and takes the form of a golden, flaring aura. The reward for defeating Totem Togedemaru and completing the trial is an Electrium Z . In Generation VII , Zing Zap was its signature move . ↑ Pokémon Sun and Moon site | Togedemaru

sections.text: Contents 1 Game data 1.1 NPC appearances 1.2 Pokédex entries 1.3 Game locations 1.3.1 In side games 1.4 Held items 1.5 Stats 1.5.1 Base stats 1.6 Type effectiveness 1.7 Learnset 1.7.1 By leveling up 1.7.2 By TM/TR 1.7.3 By breeding 1.7.4 By tutoring 1.7.5 By transfer from another generation 1.8 Side game data 1.9 Evolution data 1.10 Sprites Game data [ edit | edit source ] NPC appearances [ edit | edit source ] In Pokémon Ultra Sun and Ultra Moon , a Togedemaru nicknamed Togemaru resides at the Hokulani Observatory on Ula'ula Island . During the player's trial , Togemaru uses its Lightning Rod Ability to protect Sophocles ' Charjabug from a blast of electricity. Pokédex entries [ edit | edit source ] This Pokémon was unavailable prior to Generation VII. Generation VII Alola S M : #225 Alola US UM : #292 Kanto #— This Pokémon has no Pokédex entries in Let's Go, Pikachu! and Let's Go, Eevee! . Sun The spiny fur on its back is normally at rest. When this Pokémon becomes agitated, its fur stands on end and stabs into its attackers. Moon The long hairs on its back act as lightning rods. The bolts of lightning it attracts are stored as energy in its electric sac. Ultra Sun When it's surprised or agitated, the 14 fur spikes on its back will stand up involuntarily. Ultra Moon Its capacity to produce electricity is somewhat limited, so it gets charged up by letting lightning strike it! Generation VIII Galar #348 Isle of Armor #— Crown Tundra #— Sinnoh #— Hisui #— This Pokémon has no Pokédex entries in Brilliant Diamond, Shining Pearl , and Legends: Arceus . Sword With the long hairs on its back, this Pokémon takes in electricity from other electric Pokémon. It stores what it absorbs in an electric sac. Shield When it's in trouble, it curls up into a ball, makes its fur spikes stand on end, and then discharges electricity indiscriminately. Generation IX Paldea #— Kitakami #— Blueberry #— Lumiose #— Hyperspace #— This Pokémon has no Pokédex entries in Generation IX . Spin-off games Pokopia #— This Pokémon has no Pokédex entries in Pokopia . Togedemaru in the Alola Pokédex Game locations [ edit | edit source ] This Pokémon was unavailable prior to Generation VII. sections.title: Pokédex entries
sections.text: Pokédex entries [ edit | edit source ] Episode Pokémon Source Entry SM006 Togedemaru Rotom Pokédex Togedemaru, the Roly-Poly Pokémon. An Electric and Steel type. Its ability to generate electricity is weak, but its long needle acts as a lightning rod , so it can attract lightning and store the electricity. When happy, it forms a ball and rolls around. But once it starts rolling, it can't control itself.
